apiVersion: v1
items:
- apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: Deployment
  metadata:
    annotations:
      deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
      kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
        {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"labels":{"app":"demo-app"},"name":"demo-app","namespace":"demo"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"demo-app"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"demo-app"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["sh","-c","mkdir -p /www\necho hello \u003e /www/index.html\nhttpd -f -p 5678 -h /www\n"],"image":"busybox:1.36","name":"demo-app","ports":[{"containerPort":5678,"name":"http"}]}]}}}}
    creationTimestamp: "2026-06-23T12:39:07Z"
    generation: 1
    labels:
      app: demo-app
    name: demo-app
    namespace: demo
    resourceVersion: "863432"
    uid: 40b69096-d36e-412e-be85-9c0fdd2ee6f8
  spec:
    progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
    replicas: 1
    revisionHistoryLimit: 10
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        app: demo-app
    strategy:
      rollingUpdate:
        maxSurge: 25%
        maxUnavailable: 25%
      type: RollingUpdate
    template:
      metadata:
        creationTimestamp: null
        labels:
          app: demo-app
      spec:
        containers:
        - command:
          - sh
          - -c
          - |
            mkdir -p /www
            echo hello > /www/index.html
            httpd -f -p 5678 -h /www
          image: busybox:1.36
          imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
          name: demo-app
          ports:
          - containerPort: 5678
            name: http
            protocol: TCP
          resources: {}
          terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
          terminationMessagePolicy: File
        dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
        restartPolicy: Always
        schedulerName: default-scheduler
        securityContext: {}
        terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
  status:
    availableReplicas: 1

content.thinking:
The deployment uses label `app: demo-app` and the container listens on port 5678. The app serves only the root path "/". 

I need to:
1. Create a Service targeting pods with `app: demo-app` on port 5678
2. Create an Ingress that:
   - Uses the nginx ingress class
   - Routes `app.example.com/svc` to the service
   - Since the app only serves "/", I need to rewrite the path from `/svc` to `/`

The key here is that the app only serves "/", but the ingress needs to expose it at "/svc". 

I'll use the nginx rewrite-target annotation to handle the path rewriting, then define both the Service and Ingress resources.

content.text: The app uses label `app: demo-app` and listens on port 5678, serving only `/`. Since the ingress path is `/svc` but the app only serves `/`, I need a rewrite annotation.
